title: oreogrammitis baldwinii
intro: Oreogrammitis baldwinii, also known as the Baldwin's fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, including Hawaii. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The leaves of the Baldwin's fern can be made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
